abstract: This paper explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali and evaluates its enduring relevance within the contemporary academic landscape. A preeminent scholar in Islamic tradition, Al-Ghazali argued that education should transcend the mere transfer of information, serving instead as a vital vehicle for character development and spiritual cultivation. Utilizing a library research methodology, this study examines his insights into moral and ethical instruction to determine how his classical views can address the challenges facing modern education. Al-Ghazali advocated for learning methods that are active, reflective, and rooted in spiritual values—approaches that offer a robust framework for strengthening ethics in today’s schools. Ultimately, this research seeks to bridge traditional Islamic thought with current educational needs, identifying the transformative contributions Al-Ghazali’s legacy can offer to the modern global education system.
